{"query":"Linda Noskova Wimbledon 2026 women's singles final opponent match score","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1759,"verdict":null,"extra_info":{"type":"featured_snippet","text":"Linda Noskova has been crowned Wimbledon women's singles champion after a thrilling final victory over Karolina Muchova, winning 6-2, 5-7, 6-3.6 days ago"},"results":[{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Karol%C3%ADna_Muchov%C3%A1","title":"Karolína Muchová - Wikipedia","content":"156, reached in January 2026.
Muchová has won three WTA Tour singles titles, including a WTA 1000 event at the 2026 Qatar Open.
After winning her opening-match against Dayana Yastremska, Muchová upset two-time Grand Slam champion and 12th seed Garbiñe Muguruza in the second round to score her first top-20 victory, advancing to the third round of the tournament.
Seeded 19th at Wimbledon, she reached the quarterfinals for the second time, defeating 30th seed Paula Badosa.
2, Aryna Sabalenka in a three-set match lasting over three hours, saving a match point and recovering from 2–5 down in the final set to reach her first Grand Slam final, as the fourth-lowest-ranked women's finalist in the French Open history.
At the US Open, Muchová reached the semifinals stage of the tournament for the second consecutive year, without dropping a set, defeating Katie Volynets, Naomi Osaka, Anastasia Potapova, fifth seed Jasmine Paolini and 22nd seed Beatriz Haddad Maia.
Muchová partenered with 23-time singles major champion Serena Williams at the Berlin Open where the pair lost in the first round.
"Linda Noskova fends off Karolina Muchova fightback to win first grand slam title at Wimbledon"."},{"url":"https://www.espn.com/tennis/story/_/id/49332915/wimbledon-2026-today-blog-11-07-2026-live-updates-news-score-results-karolina-muchova-linda-noskova-all-czech-women-final-july-11#:~:text=Linda%20Noskova%20has%20been%20crowned,%2D7%2C%206%2D3.","title":"Wimbledon 2026 today: Live updates, news as Linda Noskova beats Karolina Muchova in gripping final - as it happened - ESPN","content":"Linda Noskova has been crowned Wimbledon women's singles champion after a thrilling final victory over Karolina Muchova, winning 6-2, 5-7, 6-3.
Noskova appeared to bea heading for a swift victory after winning the first set 6-3 in 31 minutes."},{"url":"https://www.nytimes.com/athletic/live-blogs/wimbledon-2026-live-updates-womens-final-score-result-latest/NatZUgorEOVs/?eafs_enabled=false","title":"How Linda Noskova beat Karolina Muchova in three sets to win first Wimbledon title - The Athletic","content":"6 days ago — Ninth seed Nosková, 21, beat fellow Czech player Karolína Muchová in three sets, 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 on Centre Court at SW19. The result, secured on ...
Nosková, 21, beat fellow Czech, Muchová, in a deciding set to win her first major title."},{"url":"https://www.usatoday.com/story/sports/tennis/wimb/2026/07/10/wimbledon-womens-final-linda-noskova-karolina-muchova-channel-time-results-highlights/90880730007/","title":"Wimbledon women's final: Linda Noskova holds off Karolina Muchova in three-set win","content":"The 21-year-old Czech needed six match points to defeat her fellow countrywoman and Olympic doubles partner Karolína Muchova, 6-2, 5-7, 6-3, on Saturday, July 11, to claim the first Grand Slam title of her career.
With the victory, Noskova (21 years, 224 days) becomes the youngest Wimbledon champion since fellow Czech Petra Kvitova (21 years, 116 days) in 2011.
She’s also the third Czech woman to win the Wimbledon women's title in four years, following Markéta Vondroušová’s win in 2023 and Barbora Krejčíková’s victory in 2024.","date":"6 days ago"},{"url":"https://www.olympics.com/en/news/wimbledon-2026-womens-singles-final-results-karolina-muchova-linda-noskova","title":"Wimbledon 2026: Linda Nosková fights off Karolína Muchová for maiden Grand Slam crown","content":"The 21-year-old ninth seed prevailed in an all-Czech women’s singles final at Wimbledon 2026 against Karolína Muchová, her Paris 2024 Olympic doubles partner.
Nosková clinched the Wimbledon crown with a 6-2, 5-7, 6-3 victory over the 10th seed on Saturday (11 July), becoming the 10th different winner of the Venus Rosewater Dish in a decade.
It has been a magnificent grass-court season for Nosková, from winning the singles and doubles titles in Berlin to now clinching the famous Venus Rosewater Dish in south-west London.
Muchová finally held after 15 points and then played a 19-point return game, where Nosková double-faulted on her fourth match point to hand her opponent her first break of the day.","date":"6 days ago"},{"url":"https://www.wtatennis.com/tournaments/wimbledon/scores/LS72320496","title":"Final The Championships, Wimbledon 2026 | WTA Official","date":"5 days ago","content":"5 days ago — N. [edit&action=edit&section=1 \"Edit section: Etymology\")]. The German name *Linde* was originally an abbreviated form of older names such as *Dietlinde* and *Sieglinde*. The name-element *Linde* is possibly derived from the same root as the linden tree, with reference to a shield made of that wood, but may have become associated with Germanic *lind* meaning \"soft, tender\", the image of the tree being used to indicate a gentle personality. Linda was at the height of popularity in the Anglosphere in the middle of the 20th century, particularly in the 1950s. In recent years, Linda has been a name that is more common for the daughters of Chinese and South Korean immigrants to the United States.","score":0.027358351,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.thelindalindas.com","title":"The Linda Lindas Official Website","content":"# image. ## Music. ## videos. ## SHOP.","score":0.023208743,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCEwkC0_dBZwR0y10NnQztlw","title":"The Linda Lindas","content":"The Linda Lindas 'Growing Up' is out now on Epitaph Records. Her charm ignites sexual tension, exposing the fragility beneath their happy facade.Self-assured Linda begins working for a wealthy Buenos Aires family. Linda is perceived by the members of the family as attractive, someone who does not seem a maid. Camilo (Rafael Spregelburd), the father of the family, is the first one to notice her appearance as someone who cannot be ignored; Luisa (Julieta Cardinali), the mother, becomes equally captivated; Ceferino (Felipe Gonzalez Otaño), the son, sees her as an opportunity to make his fantasies a reality; and Matilda (Minerva Casero), the daughter, finds in her the reason for the awakening of a dormant part inside her. Clearly, the intrafamilial dynamics are affected, and this provides the story a need to recontextualize situations that were understood to be static depictions of normalcy, for it should come as no surprise that marriage is nothing but a façade and the loss of desire is the price paid for security.","score":0.012346842,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.95,"request_id":"dc6ea9a0-3d8e-42d9-80ea-dfa74b93251d"}
